sections.paragraph: συγγενής, ές [σύν, γένος] ‘connected by lineage, related’, in NT only as noun relative, relation, kinfolk: sg. J 18:26; Ro 16:11; mostly pl. Lk 1:58; 14:12; 21:16; Ac 10:24; Ro 16:7, 21 may be understood in the same sense, but probably w. the nuance expressed in 9:3, where the focus is on Paul’s warm identification with his people Israel: all Israel constitutes his kinfolk. The dat. pl. συγγενεῦσιν appears in Mk 6:4; Lk 2:44.
